item.title: Trans Girl Tuesday
item.description:
I gotta say that I feel that transitioning is one of the most beautiful things in the world. To discover yourself and do so much work and sacrifice so much to be the person you were meant to be, it's so inspiring.
Wiggly are Juicy are dating and they are adorable.
HRT is magic, but like, for real.
She's heavily involved in the Zelda fandom, founder and creator of multiple fansites, and I think she streams too?
She's such a sweetheart, always trying to uplift others, and call out BS, including that within the trans community (lots of these posts were responses to a trans woman saying 20 was too late too start transitioning)
It's never too late, don't ever give up. Persist out of spite if you have to. I don't really know what else to say. For now this is all I can really do. I hope these posts inspire at least one person to keep pushing forward.
item.dc:creator: MermaidNinja

Paradise Found indeed ❤️
I love my wonderful partner, she is my world.
Dog tax
Edit: Woah, front page! Hello everyone!
item.dc:creator: Zinc142

item.title: It's my Soberversary!
item.description: Two years ago today I woke up with the worst hangover I had ever had. I had the spins, I was throwing up, and I was having a panic attack. I was about two and a half months out from losing my husband, who died very suddenly (although perhaps not entirely unexpectedly) from complications related to his substance use (alcohol). He was 37. In 2019, it was my best friend (many, many substances). Imgur was there to support me through both losses and I am so grateful. Nobody else has ever characterized me as an alcoholic, or said I had a problem with my drinking. Drugs? Well, that's another story. But the honest truth is that, at various times, I have had a problem with both. My spouse was a good man and a good husband. We were very in love for a very long time. And I think the best way for me to honor his life, and my own, is for me not to go down the way he did. Something will take me out eventually. But it's not going to be substance use. It has been two years since anything more mind altering than coffee entered my body. And I am pretty proud of that.
item.dc:creator: accidie

item.dc:creator: ShoemakerThe

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1250 (fluffy little bundles)
item.description:
Let's fight for our bread.
Let us fight to create a better world. The worst that could happen is that we kill a few billionaires, and then we all die anyway.
I am both of these people.
Hey so I know reading biblical scripture isn't everyone's theme here, and that there are some people who think that any aspect of religion is evil, but I've got two books from the Orthodox-Catholic Canon you might read as inspirational homework. These books are more historical than religious in nature, too: First and Second Maccabees.
(Seriously, what the fuck?)
But anyway, those two books, not found in the Protestant Canon, are about the revolt by the Jews against the Seleucid Empire, one of the most powerful successor states carved from the empire of Alexander III of Makedon. A tiny power revolted against one of the most powerful states of the Ancient World, and that revolt in the end helped lead to the utter collapse of the Seleucid Empire. These books are also where we get that whole Hanukkah story, by the way.
Seriously. You don't have a favorite dinosaur, I ain't gonna date you.
Anyway, the books of Maccabees also have some tactical advice for fighting war elephants, so there is that. It's just a shame that pikes aren't as good at fighting tanks...
Anyway, had a nice dinner last night with a friend, but my car died, probably the battery--I accidentally left my key in the ignition, and jump-starting it did nothing at all. Womp womp. Just another thing to burn money on instead of the stuff I'd like to spend it on, I guess. [Like a still. I wanted to get a still. so I could start distilling some of the mead I've made that I didn't like, so as to make potential disinfectants. And. Um. Stuff.]
Right now, I'm reading Maccabees AND Seven Pillars of Wisdom, by T.E. Lawrence. I also didn't get rid of my Byzantine military manual collection, though again, not exactly sure how useful those are going to be in the next few years. I'm also thinking about beginning a re-read of Thud!, by Terry Pratchett, though its not nearly as tactical as Night Watch.
Sir Terry Pratchett had this idea of what could only be called "militant decency." Use it. Meme forthcoming. But in this note, don't pretend to be cruel to others to fit in--it's still being cruel.
Let us turn our rage and anger into fighting for a better place. And it's okay if we have to burn the motherfucker down to start again from scratch.
Mood.
It would be nice, though I could go without the burning bushes right now until we actually get some rain.
I ended up dissociating twice today for about an hour each time. It is a cheap way to pass the time, but I really don't recommend it, and coming out of it is really quite disorienting. I mean, I really would have rather spent that time playing Disco Elysium.
I dreamed I was a TikTok wild forager, and I was teaching people how to make a coffee substitute with roasted juniper berries.
CROOOOW ATTACK
This meme isn't wrong.
I did have dreams, but now it's about survival. And [looking at you, @Ronelyn] survival itself is a perfectly acceptable act of rebellion, and if you can't do more than that, that's okay.
Really? Oh goddammit.
Well, now we know what the porn version of SpongeBob would be called.
I want to feel this against my skin.
I'd eat it.
Me, half of yesterday.
Fuck you, Kissinger.
This reads a lot like a headline about my character in a campaign I'm playing: the "wizard" Rich Scroll who is the elected mayor of Old Town, and runs his own still, carries an emergency revolver, and fucks with magic. [The Rattlesnake would be food for his familiar(s): a pair of Scrub-Jays.]
And it's only the 21st. We are going to be saying this a lot here in the States.
Yeah, this tracks.
Tag yourselves, I guess.
It's more accurate to have a T. rex in a fighter jet.
Resplendent Quetzal
Yeah, accurate.
Diatrema as a genus seems to have been resurrected
Carboniferous fishy
Bwahahahahahha
Wheeeeee.
We can have this world. Or at least, we can try and aim for it and whatever we get will be better than this shitty dystopia we're in now.
Pippin tax. Today's message from Pippin: "Whatdoin?"
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Images of Note: Elon Musk did a Nazi Salute on Stage.
item.description:
Happy Tree Friends was a special show
As someone who grew up with two different accents at home, yeah
me
Yeah
ya :D
Blessed are the droids, for without them we would not have the death star plans
EVERYONE IS PRETTY FUCKING SURE PINKIE
they have committed crimes
mlem
If they get social media they might learn how they'll be forced to have a child at 14 by Ron DeSantis.
it soggy
Yes
sound on
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging http://bsky.abbii.gay: For Bluesky http://onlyfans.abbii.gay: for Tips! Yeah, yesterday was fucked and it's going to get even more fucked as time goes on. Go get signal and keep your comms encrypted. And yes, Elon Musk did a Nazi Salute on Stage.
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: I hear Imgur likes these sorts of things
item.dc:creator: thebadhorse

item.title: I just enjoyed the movie
item.description: I honestly never really cared for any movies I'd seen him in. I'm well aware it's just my opinion too. My wife loves him. But a basic no spoiler review for the the film. It was fun. Nick had no problems poking fun at himself. Pedro Pascal, well, he was beyond funny. It's on Netflix now. Recommend. Don't expect Shakespeare. But it was a fun time. I've been down with flu for the last 4 days and am so bored I'm watching things that I have a passing interest in.
item.dc:creator: Eomund521

item.title: What hurts today - Day 39
item.description: Beast Envy... Had to use my backup meds last night. Just to try and get some sleep, cuz I stupidly let myself run out of the primary. So today will be side effect day. Headaches, vertigo, bloating. All that and my hand hurts. Woke me up twice, swollen and sore. Plus the mental strain of the new administration promoting outright Nazis. I hate this timeline.
